title: Crepidiastrum yoshinoi
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum yoshinoi is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, and it belongs to the genus Crepidiastrum. This plant is native to East Asia and is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits. Within the family Asteraceae, Crepidiastrum yoshinoi is classified as part of the tribe Cichorieae. This tribe is a group of plants that are closely related and share similar physical and reproductive characteristics. As part of the tribe Cichorieae, Crepidiastrum yoshinoi has distinct features that set it apart from other plants in the family Asteraceae.

intro: Crepidiastrum yoshinoi, also known as Yoshino clematis or Japanese clematis, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepididiastrum yoshinoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepididiastrum yoshinoi is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas and capsules, which are used to support immune function and relieve respiratory symptoms.
